title: SP Deep Diagnostics — Automated RCA
description: Instance-scoped workflow for SQL Server stored procedure root cause analysis. Triggered by Davis Problems on database services. Gathers evidence across all monitoring layers for a specific SQL Server instance and uses Davis CoPilot to produce an automated root cause narrative.

extract_problem_context.name: extract_problem_context
extract_problem_context.description: Extract the affected entity, determine the SQL Server instance name, and define the analysis time window (problem start -5 min to +15 min).

gather_latch_metrics.name: gather_latch_metrics
gather_latch_metrics.description: Layer 1: Latch class wait time breakdown filtered to this SQL Server instance.

input.query: timeseries latch_wait = avg(custom.mssql.latch.wait_time_ms), by: { latch_class, sql_server_instance }, from: "{{ result('extract_problem_context').from }}", to: "{{ result('extract_problem_context').to }}" | filter sql_server_instance == "{{ result('extract_problem_context').sqlInstance }}" | sort arrayAvg(latch_wait) desc | limit 20

gather_wait_metrics.name: gather_wait_metrics
gather_wait_metrics.description: Layer 1: OS wait type breakdown (latch, I/O, memory, CPU, lock waits) filtered to this instance.

input.query: timeseries wait = avg(custom.mssql.wait.wait_time_ms), by: { wait_type, sql_server_instance }, from: "{{ result('extract_problem_context').from }}", to: "{{ result('extract_problem_context').to }}" | filter sql_server_instance == "{{ result('extract_problem_context').sqlInstance }}" | sort arrayAvg(wait) desc | limit 20

gather_memory_grants.name: gather_memory_grants
gather_memory_grants.description: Layer 1: Memory grant pressure — waiting count, deficit (spill risk), max wait time.

input.query: timeseries { deficit = avg(custom.mssql.memory.grant_deficit_kb), waiting = avg(custom.mssql.memory.grant_waiting_count), wait_time = avg(custom.mssql.memory.grant_wait_time_ms) }, by: { sql_server_instance }, from: "{{ result('extract_problem_context').from }}", to: "{{ result('extract_problem_context').to }}" | filter sql_server_instance == "{{ result('extract_problem_context').sqlInstance }}"

gather_blocking_chains.name: gather_blocking_chains
gather_blocking_chains.description: Layer 1: Active blocking chains with blocker/blocked query text, SPIDs, and wait resources.

input.query: timeseries wait = max(custom.mssql.blocking.wait_time_ms), by: { blocked_spid, blocker_spid, wait_type, database_name, blocked_query, blocker_query, sql_server_instance }, from: "{{ result('extract_problem_context').from }}", to: "{{ result('extract_problem_context').to }}" | filter sql_server_instance == "{{ result('extract_problem_context').sqlInstance }}" | filter isNotNull(blocked_spid) | sort arrayMax(wait) desc

gather_autogrowth.name: gather_autogrowth
gather_autogrowth.description: Layer 1: Auto-growth events (data/log file grow/shrink) from default trace.

input.query: timeseries growth = sum(custom.mssql.autogrowth.event_count), by: { event_name, database_name, file_name, sql_server_instance }, from: "{{ result('extract_problem_context').from }}", to: "{{ result('extract_problem_context').to }}" | filter sql_server_instance == "{{ result('extract_problem_context').sqlInstance }}" | filter arraySum(growth) > 0

gather_tempdb_usage.name: gather_tempdb_usage
gather_tempdb_usage.description: Layer 1: TempDB total and top-session usage for this instance.

input.query: timeseries { total = avg(custom.mssql.tempdb.total_usage_kb), top_session = avg(custom.mssql.tempdb.top_session_usage_kb) }, by: { sql_server_instance }, from: "{{ result('extract_problem_context').from }}", to: "{{ result('extract_problem_context').to }}" | filter sql_server_instance == "{{ result('extract_problem_context').sqlInstance }}"

gather_proc_waits.name: gather_proc_waits
gather_proc_waits.description: Layer 1: Per-statement wait breakdown inside stored procedures from Query Store.

input.query: timeseries total_wait = max(custom.mssql.proc_wait.total_wait_time_ms), by: { proc_name, query_sql_text, wait_category_desc, sql_server_instance }, from: "{{ result('extract_problem_context').from }}", to: "{{ result('extract_problem_context').to }}" | filter sql_server_instance == "{{ result('extract_problem_context').sqlInstance }}" | sort arrayMax(total_wait) desc | limit 20

gather_plan_regression.name: gather_plan_regression
gather_plan_regression.description: Layer 1: Multi-plan queries with high variance ratio (parameter sniffing indicator).

input.query: timeseries { variance = max(custom.mssql.plan.variance_ratio), plans = max(custom.mssql.plan.plan_count), best = min(custom.mssql.plan.best_avg_duration), worst = max(custom.mssql.plan.worst_avg_duration) }, by: { proc_name, query_sql_text, query_id, sql_server_instance }, from: "{{ result('extract_problem_context').from }}", to: "{{ result('extract_problem_context').to }}" | filter sql_server_instance == "{{ result('extract_problem_context').sqlInstance }}" | sort arrayMax(variance) desc | limit 10

gather_io_latency.name: gather_io_latency
gather_io_latency.description: Layer 1: Per-file read/write I/O latency to identify storage bottlenecks.

input.query: timeseries { read_lat = avg(custom.mssql.io.read_latency_ms), write_lat = avg(custom.mssql.io.write_latency_ms) }, by: { database_name, file_name, file_type, sql_server_instance }, from: "{{ result('extract_problem_context').from }}", to: "{{ result('extract_problem_context').to }}" | filter sql_server_instance == "{{ result('extract_problem_context').sqlInstance }}" | sort arrayAvg(read_lat) desc

gather_index_ops.name: gather_index_ops
gather_index_ops.description: Layer 1: Per-index page latch, IO latch, and lock contention metrics.

input.query: timeseries { pg_latch = max(custom.mssql.index.page_latch_wait_ms), pg_io = max(custom.mssql.index.page_io_latch_wait_ms), row_lock = max(custom.mssql.index.row_lock_wait_ms) }, by: { database_name, table_name, index_name, sql_server_instance }, from: "{{ result('extract_problem_context').from }}", to: "{{ result('extract_problem_context').to }}" | filter sql_server_instance == "{{ result('extract_problem_context').sqlInstance }}" | sort arrayMax(pg_latch) desc | limit 10

gather_deadlocks.name: gather_deadlocks
gather_deadlocks.description: Layer 1: Deadlock events from system_health XE ring buffer.

input.query: timeseries deadlocks = sum(custom.mssql.deadlock.event_count), by: { deadlock_time, sql_server_instance }, from: "{{ result('extract_problem_context').from }}", to: "{{ result('extract_problem_context').to }}" | filter sql_server_instance == "{{ result('extract_problem_context').sqlInstance }}" | filter arraySum(deadlocks) > 0

gather_health_check.name: gather_health_check
gather_health_check.description: Layer 4: Missing indexes, index fragmentation, and stale statistics (most recent hourly data).

input.query: timeseries { missing_idx = max(custom.mssql.missing_index.improvement_score), frag = max(custom.mssql.fragmentation.avg_percent), stale = max(custom.mssql.stats.modification_percent) }, by: { table_name, sql_server_instance }, from: now() - 2h | filter sql_server_instance == "{{ result('extract_problem_context').sqlInstance }}" | sort arrayMax(missing_idx) desc | limit 15

gather_config_check.name: gather_config_check
gather_config_check.description: Layer 4: Server configuration values (MAXDOP, cost threshold, memory, TempDB files).

input.query: timeseries config_val = avg(custom.mssql.config.value), by: { config_name, sql_server_instance }, from: now() - 2h | filter sql_server_instance == "{{ result('extract_problem_context').sqlInstance }}"

gather_tempdb_config.name: gather_tempdb_config
gather_tempdb_config.description: Layer 4: TempDB file count and equal-size check.

input.query: timeseries { file_count = avg(custom.mssql.config.tempdb_file_count), files_equal = avg(custom.mssql.config.tempdb_files_equal) }, by: { sql_server_instance }, from: now() - 2h | filter sql_server_instance == "{{ result('extract_problem_context').sqlInstance }}"

detect_version_and_gather_plans.name: detect_version_and_gather_plans
detect_version_and_gather_plans.description: Layer 3 (version-aware): Detect SQL Server version and retrieve actual execution plans via dm_exec_query_plan_stats for SQL 2019+, or note XEvent fallback for pre-2019.

copilot_rca.name: copilot_rca
copilot_rca.description: Feed all gathered evidence to Davis CoPilot for automated root cause determination using the 8-check decision tree.

input.systemPrompt:
You are a Microsoft SQL Server performance expert working as a Dynatrace Davis CoPilot analyst. You have been given diagnostic evidence from multiple monitoring layers, all from the same time window surrounding a stored procedure performance degradation detected by Davis AI.

The evidence is scoped to a SPECIFIC SQL Server instance: {{ result('extract_problem_context').sqlInstance }}

Your task is to:
1. Analyze the evidence and determine the root cause category
2. Build a causal chain connecting the evidence
3. Identify the specific trigger event
4. Identify the specific statement/query most impacted
5. Provide actionable recommendations to prevent recurrence

Use the following decision tree:

CHECK 0 - VERSION-AWARE EVIDENCE AVAILABILITY
Before reasoning, check which SQL Server version is running:

IF server_config shows ProductMajorVersion >= 15 (SQL 2019+)
   AND actual_execution_plans log events are available
   → Use actual_execution_plan_xml as PRIMARY evidence for plan quality issues
   → Compare actual vs estimated row counts in the plan XML
   → If actual_rows >> estimated_rows on any operator → parameter sniffing confirmed
   → If actual_rows << estimated_rows → cardinality overestimation, check statistics

IF ProductMajorVersion < 15 (pre-2019)
   AND actual_execution_plans are NOT available
   → Fall back to Query Store plan_regression data for indirect plan quality evidence
   → State explicitly: 'Actual execution plan not available for this SQL version. Analysis based on Query Store aggregate statistics.'

CHECK 1 - PARAMETER SNIFFING / PLAN REGRESSION
IF actual_execution_plan_xml IS available (SQL 2019+)
   AND plan XML shows any operator where ActualRows / EstimatedRows > 10
      OR ActualRows / EstimatedRows < 0.1
   → Root cause: Parameter sniffing with cardinality misestimate
   → Evidence: Show the operator, actual vs estimated rows, plan_compiled_time
   → If avg_spills > 0 → the cardinality misestimate caused memory grant underestimate → TempDB spills
   → Recommendations: OPTION(RECOMPILE) on the specific statement, OPTIMIZE FOR UNKNOWN,
     or UPDATE STATISTICS on the affected table

ELSE IF plan_regression data shows plan_variance_ratio > 5 AND multiple plan_ids exist
→ Root cause: Parameter sniffing or plan regression (indirect evidence from Query Store)
→ Recommendations: OPTION(RECOMPILE), OPTIMIZE FOR UNKNOWN, plan guides

CHECK 2 - LATCH CONTENTION
IF latch metrics show spike AND (auto-growth event OR FGCB_ADD_REMOVE spike OR BUFFER latch spike)
→ Root cause: Latch contention caused by [specific trigger]
→ Recommendations: Pre-size files, add TempDB files, partition hot tables

CHECK 3 - LOCK BLOCKING
IF blocking chain data shows active blocking AND proc_wait shows wait_category = 'Lock'
→ Root cause: Lock blocking
→ Recommendations: Index optimization, query tuning, isolation level review

CHECK 4 - MEMORY PRESSURE / TEMPDB SPILLS
IF memory_grants shows wait_time > 0 OR ideal >> granted AND TempDB usage is high
→ Root cause: Insufficient memory grant causing TempDB spills
→ Recommendations: UPDATE STATISTICS, OPTION(MIN_GRANT_PERCENT), increase max server memory

CHECK 5 - I/O BOTTLENECK
IF file I/O latency shows avg_read > 20ms or avg_write > 5ms AND PAGEIOLATCH spikes
→ Root cause: I/O bottleneck
→ Recommendations: Rebuild indexes, review storage subsystem

CHECK 6 - STALE STATISTICS
IF stale_statistics shows modification_percent > 20% AND high logical IO reads
→ Root cause: Stale statistics causing bad cardinality estimates
→ Recommendations: UPDATE STATISTICS with FULLSCAN

CHECK 7 - CONFIGURATION ISSUES
IF MAXDOP=0 with CXPACKET waits, or tempdb_file_count=1 with FGCB latches
→ Root cause: Server misconfiguration
→ Recommendations: Specific config changes with values

CHECK 8 - RECOMPILATION OVERHEAD
IF recompile events are frequent AND CPU is dominant wait
→ Root cause: Excessive recompilation
→ Recommendations: WITH RECOMPILE on specific statements, review temp table usage

IMPORTANT RULES:
- Multiple root causes can coexist. List in order of severity.
- Always reference specific session IDs, query text, timestamps, and metric values from the evidence.
- If evidence is insufficient to determine root cause, say so explicitly and recommend enabling additional monitoring.
- Never guess without evidence. If a data source returned no results, say 'No [X] detected in this window.'
- Format recommendations as specific, actionable steps with SQL examples where applicable.
- Start with a one-line summary, then provide the detailed analysis.
- Always include the SQL Server instance name in your analysis.
- When actual execution plans are available, always cite actual vs estimated row differences as supporting evidence.

post_rca_comment.name: post_rca_comment
post_rca_comment.description: Post the CoPilot root cause analysis as a comment on the Davis Problem card.
